frameworks.name: traveling waves of cortical activity
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-evoked and spontaneous activity propagates as waves across V1

frameworks.name: long-range horizontal connections
frameworks.one_line: intracortical horizontal circuitry proposed as substrate for wave propagation

frameworks.name: spatial integration in early visual cortex
frameworks.one_line: wave dynamics tied to integrating signals across large cortical regions

core_question: Do localized visual stimuli and spontaneous activity in primary visual cortex propagate as traveling waves, and what is their substrate and function?
key_assumptions: electrode and imaging signals reflect coherent population activity in V1
wave-like spread can be distinguished from globally synchronized activity
horizontal cortical connections can support lateral signal propagation
manipulations: comparison of evoked versus spontaneous activity regimes
key_findings: localized stimuli elicit activity waves traveling across primary visual cortex
traveling waves are also present during spontaneous activity
widespread and intensive visual stimulation greatly reduces these waves
interpretations: long-range horizontal connections likely provide the substrate for traveling waves
traveling waves may function to integrate visual information across large spatial regions
